title: Release Notes & Frequently Asked Questions | Respiratory Illnesses | CDC

February 21, 2025 release 
Major Updates: 
1. Additional Respiratory Illnesses added to Weekly National Summary
What:  
We are expanding the Weekly National Summary by including updates on additional respiratory illnesses of concern, such as pneumonia. This addition aims to provide a more comprehensive overview of respiratory illness across the nation. 
The new section, called "Additional Respiratory Illnesses," distinguishes these findings from those about COVID-19, influenza, and RSV. 
Why:  
By incorporating data on other respiratory illnesses, we aim to improve situational awareness and provide users with valuable insights into emerging health concerns. 
2. Updates to Your Community Snapshot to Improve Clarity
Update #1: Updated Wastewater Data Source Text 
What: The text regarding the data source for wastewater information has been revised for clarity on the Your Community Snapshot page. 
Why: This update is intended to increase user understanding of the data source. 
Update #2: Label Change from "Minimal" to "Very Low" 
What: We changed the label from "minimal" to "very low" for the level descriptions on the Your Community Snapshot page. 
Why: This change improves clarity and better communicate
